timeline.label: FDA 510(k) clearance for G7 acetabular hip system; expands revision-capable hip portfolio
Q1 2026: net sales $2.09B (+2.9% organic CC); $250M buybacks; raised adjusted EPS and FCF guidance
Bryan Thornal appointed Group President, Global Businesses and Americas (from Nevro CEO role)
Ivan Tornos appointed Chairman of the Board (CEO since August 2023)
Closed acquisition of Paragon 28 (~$1.39B net cash); expands foot and ankle / SET franchise
Issued $2.49B senior long-term debt; repaid $863M debt; funded Paragon and refinancing
Multi-year initiative to convert substantial U.S. independent sales agents to direct employees
2024 Investor Day: strategy for above-market growth; robotics and data analytics emphasis
Ivan Tornos appointed President and CEO; succeeded prior leadership
Zimmer Biomet spin/combination legacy integration largely complete; focus shifts to organic growth and robotics
Zimmer Holdings and Biomet merger created Zimmer Biomet; scale in knees and hips

industry.structure: Global orthopedic implants oligopoly: ZBH, Stryker, DePuy Synthes, and Smith+Nephew dominate knees and hips; hospital GPO contracting and surgeon preference create high entry barriers but persistent pricing pressure.
industry.share_shift: Robotics-enabled knee systems (ROSA, Mako, VELYS) and ambulatory site-of-care capabilities are share battlegrounds; ZBH gaining SET exposure via Paragon 28 while converting U.S. sales force.
industry.trend: Aging demographics support procedure growth; EU MDR recertification, ASC migration, and hospital cost containment compress pricing; AI, robotics, and smart implants are the innovation vector.
